<|10.76|> The deal with signs is they usually have only one meaning and really only one meaning and<|17.30|>

<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Throughout the city of Denver, for example, there are countless numbers of signs, perhaps the most common of which is a red stop sign or a red traffic light.<|13.10|>
<|13.28|> And that color alerts us to one thing and one thing only.<|18.10|>
<|20.52|> And good drivers, of course, do just that.<|22.90|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.08|> Symbols, on the other hand, are the total opposite.<|4.26|>

<|13.06|> And the deeper you get, the more you find.<|14.94|>
<|15.04|> It's so complex, a symbol is so complex that it can even carry contradictory meanings within itself.<|22.66|>
